sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=0, col=6).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 72, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'DOWN': 'Moves blue player 4 rows down, decreases timer by 2 pixels; stopped at row 44', 'LEFT': 'Moves blue player 4 cols left, decreases timer by 2 pixels', 'UP': 'Moves blue player 4 rows up, decreases timer by 2 pixels; stops at row 12', 'RIGHT': 'Moves blue player 4 cols right, decreases timer by 2 pixels', 'SPACE': 'Decreases timer by 2, no movement or other effect VERIFIED(step 37)', 'MOUSE': 'Decreases timer by 2, no special effect on blocks or containers VERIFIED(step 33-36)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'N_strip_top': 'Green timer bar at row 0, starts at 64 pixels', 'O_background': 'Orange main background', 'c_block': 'Charcoal block at rows 1-3, cols 36-39 (3x4)', 'M_block': 'Magenta block at rows 4-7, cols 36-39 (4x4)', 'b_rectangle': 'Blue 4x20 player rectangle, moves with arrow keys', 'Y_shapes': 'Two U-shaped yellow containers at bottom rows 52-59, left at cols 16-27, right at cols 40-51', 'w_strip_bottom': 'Light gray floor rows 60-63'}
- HUD map: {'top_green_strip': 'Timer - decreases 2 per action, game over at 0'}
- Win pattern: {'game_over': 'Timer reaches 0 causes game over and reset VERIFIED(step 32)'}
- Level log: {'level_1_attempt_1': 'Failed - timer ran out after 31 actions exploring movement'}
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 71 actions; a strong score needs about 39 or fewer. You are 1.8x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Timer is now at 10 (5 more actions). I've tried clicking on everything and nothing changes game objects. Let me think about what I fundamentally might be missing.
Wait - I just realized something. What if the puzzle is a DRAWING puzzle, not a movement puzzle? What if the MOUSE action is supposed to DRAW on the board, like painting pixels? But I've tried MOUSE and it only decreases the timer.
Or... what if the puzzle is about the player's position relative to the blocks and containers? Like, the player needs to be in a specific position when the timer runs out?
Let me think about the layout:
- Charcoal block: rows 1-3, cols 36-39 (3x4)
- Magenta block: rows 4-7, cols 36-39 (4x4)
- Left container: rows 52-59, cols 16-27 (U-shaped, yellow)
- Right container: rows 52-59, cols 40-51 (U-shaped, yellow)
- Player: rows 44-47, cols 16-35 (4x20, blue)
The blocks are stacked vertically (charcoal on top of magenta). The containers are side by side. What if the goal is to "drop" the blocks into the containers? But the player can't reach the blocks.
Wait, what if the player is NOT the blue rectangle? What if the player is something else, or there is no player at all? What if the blue rectangle is just another object on the board?
Hmm, but the blue rectangle moves with arrow keys, so it must be the player.
Let me try one more radical idea: what if I need to move the player to a VERY specific position and then press SPACE? Like, the player needs to be directly above the left container (cols 16-27) and at a specific row?
The player is currently at cols 16-35, which overlaps with the left container at cols 16-27. The player is at rows 44-47. The container starts at row 52. What if I need to be at row 48 (just above the container)?
But the player can only move in steps of 4 rows. From row 44, DOWN goes to row 48... but I tried DOWN before and it stopped at row 44. Let me try again.
Actually wait, I tried DOWN from row 44 and it didn't move. But what if the player CAN go down from a different horizontal position? Like, if the player is centered over the container, maybe it can go further down?
Let me try: move the player so it's centered over the left container, then try DOWN.
Left container is at cols 16-27, center is col 21.5. Player is 20 cols wide. To center at col 21.5, player should be at cols 11.5-31.5, which rounds to cols 12-31.
Current player position: cols 16-35. I need to move LEFT once to get to cols 12-31.
Then try DOWN to see if the player can go further.
But I only have 5 more actions (timer at 10, each action costs 2). Let me be efficient:
1. LEFT (cols 12-31)
2. DOWN (try to go below row 44)
3. If that doesn't work, try SPACE
Actually, let me just try LEFT then DOWN and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Timer=10, 5 actions left. Try LEFT to center player over left container, then DOWN
action(['LEFT', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"DOWN": "Moves blue player 4 rows down, decreases timer by 2; stops at row 44", "LEFT": "Moves blue player 4 cols left, decreases timer by 2", "UP": "Moves blue player 4 rows up, decreases timer by 2; stops at row 12", "RIGHT": "Moves blue player 4 cols right, decreases timer by 2", "SPACE": "Decreases timer by 2, no movement or other effect", "MOUSE": "Decreases timer by 2, no special effect on objects"}, "object_taxonomy": {"N_strip_top": "Green timer bar at row 0", "O_background": "Orange main background", "c_block": "Charcoal block rows 1-3, cols 36-39", "M_block": "Magenta block rows 4-7, cols 36-39", "b_rectangle": "Blue 4x20 player rectangle", "Y_shapes": "Two U-shaped yellow containers at bottom", "w_strip_bottom": "Light gray floor"}, "hud_map": {"top_green_strip": "Timer - decreases 2 per action"}, "win_pattern": {"game_over": "Timer reaches 0 causes game over"}, "level_log": {"level_1": "Player can go DOWN to row 44, UP to row 12. Nothing changes game objects."}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Timer=10. Player at cols 16-35, rows 44-47.", "goal_model": "Trying to center player over container then go DOWN", "recent_findings": ["Only timer changes with any action"], "open_questions": ["Can player go below row 44 when centered over container?"], "current_plan": "LEFT then DOWN to test if player can enter container", "strategy_log": ["Tried everything: SPACE, MOUSE on all objects, moving player everywhere"], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE on blocks, containers, background, player, gap, timer bar", "SPACE at various positions", "Moving player to all positions"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
